ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific mocha latte subclass exists. Mapped to closest parent class FOODON_03301638 (coffee beverage).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers coffee and coffee substitutes as a category; no mocha-specific code exists. This is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for mocha latte as a food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard medium serving of mocha latte is approximately 240 ml (240 g), equivalent to a regular café cup containing espresso, steamed milk, and chocolate syrup.

nutrient_source.title: Mocha Latte — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mocha Latte (cr_id G00010971). All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and is set to -1.

gi_source.title: Latte (café latte, milk-based coffee beverage) — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database; milk GI ~27–34, chocolate milk GI ~34–43; mocha latte estimated at GI 41 based on milk-dominant composition with added sugar from chocolate syrup
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for mocha latte exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 41 (low category) based on the closest analogous beverages: full-fat milk (GI ~27), chocolate milk (GI ~34–43), and café latte (GI ~30–42). The beverage is milk-dominant with moderate added sugar from chocolate syrup. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (41) × carbohydrate per serving (10.4 g from 240 g × 4.35 g/100g) / 100 = 4.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar from chocolate syrup; sodium 35 mg/100 g; potassium 152 mg/100 g below the 200 mg offset threshold; sweetened flavoured beverage classification
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular mocha latte due to its sweetened flavoured beverage status with added sugar and sub-threshold potassium; switching to an unsweetened plain latte or black coffee is recommended to avoid the added-sugar load while retaining the blood-pressure-neutral profile of plain coffee.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with added chocolate syrup; sugar 4.06 g/100 g (approximately 9.7 g per 240 ml serving); triggers SSB hard gate for diabetes management
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid mocha latte as it is a sugar-sweetened flavoured beverage with added chocolate syrup that raises postprandial blood glucose; a plain unsweetened latte or black coffee with no added sugar is the recommended alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar from chocolate syrup; cholesterol 7 mg/100 g; saturated fat data not available from HPB source; moderate energy density at 43 kcal/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit mocha latte consumption due to its added-sugar content from chocolate syrup, which contributes to dyslipidaemia risk; choosing an unsweetened plain latte reduces added-sugar exposure while preserving the potential cardioprotective polyphenol benefit of coffee.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sweetened chocolate syrup negates anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of cocoa; added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid mocha latte made with sweetened chocolate syrup as the added sugar negates the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of cocoa; unsweetened cocoa powder in a plain latte would provide genuine polyphenol benefit without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mocha Latte is a popular espresso-based coffee beverage combining a shot of espresso with steamed milk and sweetened chocolate syrup (or cocoa-based sauce), served hot or iced. The drink is milk-dominant, giving it a creamy texture and mild coffee flavour balanced by the sweetness and slight bitterness of chocolate. It is widely available in cafés across Singapore and internationally. Per 100 g, mocha latte provides 43 kcal, 2.81 g protein, 1.61 g fat, 4.35 g carbohydrate (of which 4.06 g is sugar), 35 mg sodium, 105 mg calcium, and 152 mg potassium. The dietary fibre content is 0.0 g/100 g (confirmed zero per HPB source). The beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened flavoured beverage due to the addition of chocolate syrup containing added sugar. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes (SSB hard gate), hypertension (sweetened flavoured beverage gate, potassium below 200 mg offset threshold), and rheumatoid arthritis (added sugar negates cocoa polyphenol benefit). For cardiovascular disease, moderate concern applies due to added sugar contributing to dyslipidaemia risk. The calcium content (105 mg/100 g) reflects the milk base and may offer a minor bone-health benefit. A standard serving is approximately 240 ml. Suitable alternatives for health-conscious consumers include plain unsweetened latte or black coffee.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a mocha latte?
Is mocha latte suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of mocha latte?
Mocha latte vs plain latte calories
Is mocha latte a sugar-sweetened beverage?
Can I drink mocha latte if I have rheumatoid arthritis?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010971) with confidence 0.95 for all reported macros and micronutrients. The HPB record is an exact match for this food item. Saturated fat and detailed fatty acid breakdown are not reported by HPB and are set to -1. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database comparators (milk-based coffee beverages) and is clearly documented as such.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g/100 g — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mocha Latte; this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this item and no supplementary approved source was available to fill them. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 41, low category) based on milk-dominant coffee beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct mocha latte GI entry exists in any approved GI source. Glycemic load per serving = 41 × (240 × 4.35/100) / 100 = 4.3. The beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened flavoured beverage (SSB) due to the addition of sweetened chocolate syrup. Multilingual local names are provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as per G6 rules, even for Western dishes commonly consumed in Singapore. No ontology code was invented; FoodOn and FoodEx2 entries are approximate matches to the closest parent classes.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as per G5 hard gate requirements.
Populated local_names for Chinese_simplified, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singaporean dishes.
Added Tamil and Hindi aliases to aliases_by_language and the flat aliases array to satisfy Q8 alias coverage requirements.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
